content.problemStatement:
Given two arrays AAA and BBB of length NNN each.

You can do the following operation on the array BBB **exactly once**:


Chose two integers RRR (1‚â§R‚â§N)(1 \le R \le N)(1‚â§R‚â§N) and K(K‚â•0)K(K \ge 0)K(K‚â•0) and apply KKK right-rotations to the subarray [1,R][1,R][1,R].

Applying a right-rotation to the array C=[C1,C2,C3,‚Ä¶,CM]C = [C_1, C_2, C_3, \ldots, C_M]C=[C1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,C3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CM‚Äã] changes it to [CM,C1,C2,‚Ä¶,C(M‚àí1)][C_M, C_1, C_2, \ldots, C_{(M-1)}][CM‚Äã,C1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,C(M‚àí1)‚Äã]
Find whether you can transform the array BBB into the array AAA by applying the mentioned operation **exactly** once. If it is possible, find the **minimum** value of KKK for which it is possible.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains the integer NNN - the size of arrays AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã - the elements of the array AAA.
The third line contains NNN integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã - the elements of the array BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line, the **minimum** possible value of KKK. If it is not possible to convert BBB to AAA, print `-1`.
constraints: Sum of NNN across all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Let R=3R = 3R=3 and K=1K = 1K=1. Thus, we apply 111 right-rotation on the subarray [4,5,1][4, 5, 1][4,5,1]. The rotated subarray is [1,4,5][1, 4, 5][1,4,5]. The updated array is [1,4,5,2][1, 4, 5, 2][1,4,5,2] which is equal to the array AAA.
It can be shown that the array BBB cannot be converted into the array AAA using KKK less than 111.
Test case 222: The array BBB cannot be converted to the array AAA using the mentioned operation exactly once.
